title: Elaphoglossum melanolepis
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum melanolepis is classified as a species within the genus Elaphoglossum, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. The class is further grouped under the division Pteridophyta. The kingdom to which Elaphoglossum melanolepis belongs is Plantae. It is a type of fern, a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Elaphoglossum melanolepis, a type of fern, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin damage. Studies have identified the bioactive constituents of E. melanolepis to include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
